name: CT Angiography Head
description: CT angiography of the intracranial arteries for evaluation of aneurysm, stenosis, or vascular malformation

findings.technique: CT angiography was performed following intravenous contrast administration. Images were acquired from the aortic arch to the vertex with thin-slice reconstructions and 3D volume-rendered images.
findings.comparison: No prior studies available for comparison.

sections.name: Aortic Arch and Great Vessels
sections.content: The aortic arch demonstrates [normal configuration / arch anomaly]. The brachiocephalic trunk, left common carotid, and left subclavian arteries originate normally. No significant stenosis or aneurysm.

sections.name: Carotid Bifurcations
sections.content: The bilateral carotid bifurcations are [patent / show mild atherosclerotic disease]. No focal stenosis or dissection.

sections.name: Intracranial Internal Carotid Arteries
sections.content: The intracranial ICAs demonstrate [normal caliber and course / no aneurysm / no stenosis / no dissection]. The carotid terminus is normal.

sections.name: Anterior Cerebral Arteries
sections.content: The A1 segments of the bilateral anterior cerebral arteries are [present / hypoplastic]. The A2 segments are [normal / show fenestration]. No aneurysm.

sections.name: Middle Cerebral Arteries
sections.content: The M1 segments of the bilateral middle cerebral arteries demonstrate [normal caliber / no stenosis / no aneurysm]. The M2 branches are [symmetric / show variant anatomy].

sections.name: Posterior Cerebral Arteries
sections.content: The P1 segments of the bilateral posterior cerebral arteries are [present / hypoplastic]. The P2-P4 segments are [normal / show fenestration]. No aneurysm.

sections.name: Vertebrobasilar System
sections.content: The vertebral arteries demonstrate [symmetric caliber / left/right dominance / hypoplasia]. The basilar artery is [normal / shows fenestration]. No aneurysm or stenosis.

sections.name: Circle of Willis
sections.content: The circle of Willis is [complete / incomplete]. The anterior communicating artery is [present / absent]. The posterior communicating arteries are [patent / show fetal configuration].

sections.name: Venous Sinuses
sections.content: The dural venous sinuses including the superior sagittal, transverse, and sigmoid sinuses are [patent / show hypoplasia / thrombosis]

impression:
1. Normal CT angiography of the head.
2. No aneurysm, stenosis, or vascular malformation identified.
3. The circle of Willis is anatomically complete.
author: RadReport Templates
